stories.title: Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization
stories.description: President Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization and bomb its power plants by Tuesday night. Online, he said he agreed to suspend the bombing of Iran for two weeks.

stories.title: Congressional Democrats raise alarm over Trump's comments on Iran
stories.description: Dozens of congressional Democrats raised alarm Tuesday over President Trump's rhetoric about Iran. Most Republican lawmakers have been silent.

stories.title: Trump's deadline for an Iran deal looms. And, Artemis II crew begins the journey home
stories.description: In a press conference last night, Trump reiterated threats against Iran if the country doesn't accept a deal by 8:00 p.m. ET tonight. And, the Artemis II crew are on their way back to Earth.

stories.title: Trump backs down from imminent threat to annihilate Iran, announcing 2-week ceasefire
stories.description: After the U.S. and Iran arrived at a ceasefire agreement, President Trump suspended his threat of wide-scale destruction of Iran's civilian and military infrastructure.

stories.title: Beer cans, helium balloons and mortgages: An unexpected mix of things affected by war
stories.description: It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.

stories.title: Trump provides details behind the dramatic rescue of an American airman trapped in Iran
stories.description: President Trump revealed many of the dramatic details on how the U.S. military scrambled to rescue two members of fighter jet that was shot down deep inside Iran.

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson to be released after kidnap in Iraq, militia says
stories.description: Kataib Hezbollah says the release comes on condition that she leave Iraq immediately.

stories.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in jail
stories.description: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris are on their way home after being allowed to leave the country, the French president says.

stories.title: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
stories.description: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.

stories.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
stories.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
stories.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

stories.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: 'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
stories.description: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Trump agrees to suspend attack on Iran for two weeks
Iran positively reviewing Pakistan’s request for a two-week ceasefire, source says
stories.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is aware ​of ‌Pakistan's ⁠proposal for a ‌two-week extension to a ⁠deadline he imposed on Iran, ​and a ‌response will come, White House press ‌secretary Karoline Leavitt ​said on Tuesday. "The President has been ​made aware ​of the ​proposal, and a res...

stories.title: Trump repeats falsehoods that he predicted 9/11 and demanded Bin Laden's death
stories.description: During a press conference on Monday about the war in Iran, Donald Trump made a series of false claims about Washington’s progress in the conflict, his own past foreign policy, and that his book released in 2000 predicted 9/11.

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran war can now 'spiral out of control'
stories.description: As Donald Trump threatened to wipe out an entire civilisation in Iran ahead of his latest deadline for Tehran to open up the Strait of Hormuz, Qatar's foreign ministry official warned that the world was nearing a point beyond which the war would be out of anyone's control. Oil ma...

stories.title: Trump's 'rhetoric itself could create a norm' for others to terrorise entire populations
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Negah Angha, Visiting Fellow at King's College London, Former Senior Advisor at the US Department of State and National Security Council under the Biden administration, says that "the rhetoric itself, the havoc that it's causing inside Iran an...

stories.title: Are war crimes being committed in the Middle East war?
stories.description: With US and Israeli strikes on Iran's critical infrastructure already underway Tuesday amid threats of more to come, the UN and other organisations have warned that such attacks could constitute war crimes.

stories.title: An 'eerie still' in Tehran amid Trump's apocalyptic threats
stories.description: Reporting from Tehran for FRANCE 24, Reza Sayah describes an "eerie still" in the Iranian capital ahead of the expiration of the ultimatum set by US President Donald Trump, saying that one "can sense that people are on edge". Trump earlier threatened that a “whole civilization wi...

stories.title: French nationals leave Iran after more than three years in detention
stories.description: Two French nationals, who spent more than three years in an Iranian prison on espionage charges, headed home from the war-stricken country on Tuesday, President Emmanuel Macron said. Cecile Kohler, 41, and Jacques Paris, 72, had been under house arrest at the French embassy in Te...

stories.title: Trump warns a 'whole civilization will die tonight' if a deal with Iran isn't reached
stories.description: U.S. President Donald Trump threatened Tuesday that a “whole civilization will die tonight” if Iran fails to meet his latest deadline to strike a deal that includes reopening the Strait of Hormuz, while the Islamic Republic urged young people to form human chains around power pla...

stories.title: Iran-backed Iraqi armed group release kidnapped US journalist
stories.description: An Iran-backed Iraqi armed group has released an American journalist abducted in Baghdad last week, an Iraqi official said on Tuesday. The group had earlier agreed to release the journalist in a gesture of goodwill toward Iraq’s outgoing prime minister.

stories.title: 🔴 Middle East war live: Russia, China veto UN resolution on reopening Strait of Hormuz
Iran says talks with US will begin in Pakistan’s Islamabad on Friday
stories.description: Tehran says the negotiations will be based on its 10-point proposal, which calls for control over Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran agrees to open Strait of Hormuz for two-week US ceasefire
stories.description: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says ‘if attacks against Iran are halted,’ then Iran agrees to a US ceasefire.

stories.title: White House confirms two-week suspension of strikes on Iran
stories.description: The White House has confirmed the US has agreed to suspend all bombing and military attacks on Iran for two weeks.

stories.title: Trump agrees to pause attacks on Iran if Strait of Hormuz opens
stories.description: US President Donald Trump says he has agreed to extend his threatened deadline to attack Iran by two weeks.

stories.title: Trump announces two-week ceasefire as Iran agrees to reopen Hormuz Strait
stories.description: Iran has said that safe passage through Hormuz will be possible through coordination with its armed forces.

stories.title: What we know about Pakistan’s proposed Iran war pause
stories.description: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif says diplomatic efforts to resolve the US-Israeli war on Iran are progressing.

stories.title: Iranian musician stages protest at power station over Trump threats
stories.description: Iranian musician Ali Ghamsari has been performing outside the Damavand power station in a symbolic protest.

stories.title: Djibouti and the Horn of Africa: new front in the war on Iran
stories.description: Djibouti built its economy on the strategy of charging rent to different military world powers.

stories.title: Iran warns region and beyond as tension builds ahead of US ultimatum
stories.description: Trump has threatened to step up the US-Israeli war on Iran by destroying every bridge and power plant in the country.

stories.title: Pakistan appeals to Trump to extend deadline, Iran to reopen Hormuz Strait
stories.description: PM Sharif cites progress in diplomatic efforts for peaceful settlement of war, urges to allow diplomacy run its course.

stories.title: Iranians brace for possible devastation as Trump’s deadline looms
stories.description: Anxiety grips Iran with looming threats of US-led strikes targeting civilian sites, in violation of international law.

stories.title: How is the US-Israel war on Iran affecting Russian-Iranian relations?
stories.description: Moscow has been benefitting from the higher oil prices while its ally is under attack.

stories.title: Russia and China block UN resolution on Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: A total 11 out of 15 members supported resolution, which was already watered down to evade vetoes.

stories.title: Pope Leo slams Trump’s threat that Iran’s ‘civilisation will die’
stories.description: Pope Leo condemned Trump’s threats that Iran’s “civilisation will die”, calling the remarks “unacceptable".

stories.title: Trump announces two-week bilateral ceasefire with Iran
stories.description: The US has "already met and exceeded all Military objectives" and is "very far" advanced toward a long-term peace deal, the president says

stories.title: Israeli military reports another missile launch from Iran
stories.description: This is the second missile launch after the announcement of a US-Iranian ceasefire

stories.title: Vance, Witkoff, Kushner expected at US-Iran talks in Pakistan — CNN
stories.description: Pakistan agreed to mediate the negotiations at the invitation of Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif

stories.title: IDF says started to intercept another Iranian missile launch
stories.description: The statement was published after an announcement of a ceasefire with Iran was made public

stories.title: US in intense talks on Iran — US leader
stories.description: President Donald Trump also says he expects a briefing on Pakistan’s proposal to be held soon

stories.title: Petrochemical plant in Iran’s Mahshahr comes under attack — news agency
stories.description: In a separate report, Press TV says that Israel and the United States struck an aluminum plant in Arak

stories.title: Carlson urges US military to disobey presidential order to strike Iran
stories.description: The journalist warns that the world is at a "pivot point" after which "nothing is the same"

stories.title: US yet to answer Pakistan’s proposal to extend deadline for deal with Iran — White House
stories.description: Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt was quoted as saying that the president was aware of the proposal

stories.title: Iran launches Wave 99 of its operation targeting US, Israel — IRGC
stories.description: The military of the Islamic republic struck a number of targets in the UAE and Kuwait

stories.title: Shiite radicals release US journalist Kittleson abducted in Iraq
stories.description: Kataib Hezbollah demanded that the journalist leave the country immediately

stories.title: Bahraini UNSC resolution jeopardized prospects for talks with Iran — Russian diplomat
stories.description: Vasily Nebenzya says that a unilateral resolution would also hinder peace initiatives undertaken by China, Pakistan, and Turkey

stories.title: Russia, China offer well-balanced UNSC draft resolution on Iran — diplomat
stories.description: Vasily Nebenzya stressed that free navigation in the Strait of Hormuz was attainable only through ending hostilities and reaching a negotiated solution

stories.title: Israeli gas fields included in list of possible targets for IRGC attacks — agency
stories.description: According to the report by Fars, the Karish and Tanin gas fields "play a key role in the energy supply" of Israel

stories.title: Bahraini UNSC draft resolution would have greenlit aggression against Iran — diplomat
stories.description: The draft resolution was not passed by the UN Security Council as it was supported by 11 members, while Russia and China voted against and Pakistan and Colombia abstained
